settings.title: Settings
settings.providerManagedByOrg: Provider options are managed by your organization's remote configuration
settings.autoFetchModelList: The extension automatically fetches the latest Cline model list. If you're unsure which model to choose, Cline works best with
settings.thinkingEnabledByDefault: Thinking is enabled by default for this model.
settings.reasoningEffortDescription: Higher effort improves depth, but uses more tokens.
settings.adaptiveThinkingDescription: Use None to disable adaptive thinking. Higher effort increases response detail and token usage.
settings.compactPromptDescription: A system prompt optimized for smaller context window (e.g. 8k or less).
settings.compactPromptNoMcpFocus: Does not support Mcp and Focus Chain
settings.preferredLanguageDescription: The language that Cline should use for communication.
settings.terminalOutputLimitDescription: Maximum number of lines to include in terminal output when executing commands. When exceeded, lines will be removed from the middle, saving tokens.
settings.remotelyConfiguredMessage: This setting is managed by your organization's remote configuration
settings.useDifferentModels: Use different models for Plan and Act modes
settings.useDifferentModelsDescription: Switching between Plan and Act mode will persist the API and model used in the previous mode. This may be helpful e.g. when using a strong reasoning model to architect a plan for a cheaper coding model to act on.

settingsSections.telemetryDescription: Help improve Cline by sending usage data and error reports. No code, prompts, or personal information are ever sent. See our
settingsSections.disableBrowserDescription: Prevent Cline from using browser actions (e.g. launch, click, type).
settingsSections.viewportSizeDescription: Set the size of the browser viewport for screenshots and interactions.
settingsSections.remoteBrowserDescription: Enable Cline to use your Chrome
settingsSections.remoteBrowserRequiresDebug: . You can specify a custom path below. Using a remote browser connection requires starting Chrome in debug mode
settingsSections.remoteBrowserDebugOrButton: or using the button below. Enter the host address or leave it blank for automatic discovery.
settingsSections.customBrowserArgsDescription: Space-separated arguments to pass to the browser executable.
settingsSections.defaultTerminalProfileDescription: Select the default terminal Cline will use. 'Default' uses your VSCode global setting.
settingsSections.shellIntegrationDescription: Set how long Cline waits for shell integration to activate before executing commands. Increase this value if you experience terminal connection timeouts.
settingsSections.terminalReuseDescription: When enabled, Cline will reuse existing terminal windows that aren't in the current working directory. Disable this if you experience issues with task lockout after a terminal command.
settingsSections.terminalExecutionModeDescription: Choose whether Cline runs commands in the VS Code terminal or a background process.
settingsSections.resetStateDescription: This will reset all global state and secret storage in the extension.
settingsSections.aboutDescription: An AI assistant that can use your CLI and Editor. Cline can handle complex software development tasks step-by-step with tools that let him create & edit files, explore large projects, use the browser, and execute terminal commands (after you grant permission).

featureSettings.subagentsDescription: Let Cline run focused subagents in parallel to explore the codebase for you.
featureSettings.nativeToolCallDescription: Use native function calling when available
featureSettings.strictPlanModeDescription: Prevents file edits while in Plan mode
featureSettings.featureTipsDescription: Show rotating tips during the thinking phase to help you discover Cline features.
featureSettings.backgroundEditDescription: Allow edits without stealing editor focus
featureSettings.checkpointsDescription: Save progress at key points for easy rollback
featureSettings.clineWebToolsDescription: Access web browsing and search capabilities
featureSettings.worktreesDescription: Enables git worktree management for running parallel Cline tasks.
featureSettings.yoloModeDescription: Execute tasks without user's confirmation. Auto-switches from Plan to Act mode and disables the ask question tool. Use with extreme caution.
featureSettings.doubleCheckCompletionDescription: Rejects the first completion attempt and asks the model to re-verify its work against the original task requirements before accepting.
featureSettings.lazyTeammateModeDescription: Sometimes Cline just isn't feeling it today. For entertainment purposes only.
featureSettings.hooksDescription: Enable lifecycle and tool hooks during task execution.
featureSettings.mcpDisplayModeDescription: Controls how MCP responses are displayed

remoteConfig.flushedBuffers: Flushed buffers! Please check the output channel for more detailed information
remoteConfig.settingsManagedByOrg: These settings are managed by your organization's remote configuration.
remoteConfig.optedOut: You have opted out of remote config. Opt back in to apply it and see it here.
remoteConfig.notConfiguredYet: You haven't configured remote config yet. Do so through our

commonFields.apiKeyStoredLocally: This key is stored locally and only used to make API requests from this extension.
commonFields.getApiKeySignup: You can get a {{provider}} API key by signing up here.
commonFields.providerRoutingDefault: Load balance across providers (AWS, Google Vertex, etc.), prioritizing price while considering uptime
commonFields.providerRoutingPrice: Sort by price, prioritizing the lowest cost provider
commonFields.providerRoutingThroughput: Sort by throughput, prioritizing highest throughput (may increase cost)
commonFields.providerRoutingLatency: Sort by response time, prioritizing lowest latency

openaiCompatible.name: OpenAI Compatible
openaiCompatible.noteComplexPrompts: Note: Cline uses complex prompts and works best with Claude models. Less capable models may not work as expected.

openRouter.name: OpenRouter
openRouter.keyStoredLocally: This key is stored locally and only used to make API requests from this extension.

bedrock.awsProfileCredentials: Using AWS Profile credentials from ~/.aws/credentials. Leave profile name empty to use the default profile. These credentials are only used locally to make API requests from this extension.
bedrock.awsKeyCredentials: Authenticate by either providing the keys above or use the default AWS credential providers, i.e. ~/.aws/credentials or environment variables. These credentials are only used locally to make API requests from this extension.
bedrock.customModelDescription: Select "Custom" when using the Application Inference Profile in Bedrock. Enter the Application Inference Profile ARN in the Model ID field.

vertex.setupInstructions: To use Google Cloud Vertex AI, you need to
vertex.step1: 1) create a Google Cloud account › enable the Vertex AI API › enable the desired Claude models,
vertex.step2: 2) install the Google Cloud CLI › configure Application Default Credentials.

ollama.name: Ollama
ollama.optionalApiKey: Optional API key for authenticated Ollama instances or cloud services. Leave empty for local installations.
ollama.unableToFetch: Unable to fetch models from Ollama server. Please ensure Ollama is running and accessible, or enter the model ID manually above.
ollama.requestTimeoutDescription: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for API responses before timing out.
ollama.ollamaInstructions: Ollama allows you to run models locally on your computer. For instructions on how to get started, see their
ollama.noteComplexPrompts: Note: Cline uses complex prompts and works best with Claude models. Less capable models may not work as expected.

together.noteComplexPrompts: Note: Cline uses complex prompts and works best with Claude models. Less capable models may not work as expected.

xai.noteComplexPrompts: Note: Cline uses complex prompts and works best with Claude models. Less capable models may not work as expected.

nousresearch.name: NousResearch
nousresearch.noteComplexPrompts: Note: Cline uses complex prompts and works best with Claude models. Less capable models may not work as expected.

dify.name: Dify
dify.noteModelSelection: Note: The model selection is handled within your Dify application configuration.

oca.failedRefreshModels: Failed to refresh models. Check your session or network.

account.title: Account
account.signUpDescription: Sign up for an account to get access to the latest models, billing dashboard to view usage and credits, and more upcoming features.
account.agreeToTerms: By continuing, you agree to the
account.cannotChangeWithRemoteConfig: This cannot be changed while your organization has remote configuration enabled.

onboarding.selectOption: Select an option below to get started.
onboarding.completeInBrowser: Complete account creation in your browser. Then come back here to finish up.
onboarding.bringOwnKeyDescription: Use Cline with your provider of choice
onboarding.canChangeLater: You can change this later in settings
